query: What does the CSS margin property do in production?

competitors.label: margin
competitors.selectionReason: This is the canonical MDN reference for the margin property, including shorthand behavior, auto margins, negative margins, and margin collapsing.
theirStrengths: MDN is authoritative on margin syntax, longhand versus shorthand behavior, auto margins, and collapse rules.
theirGaps: The reference is less interview-shaped and less explicit about when margin is the wrong spacing primitive compared with padding or gap.

realWorldUseCases: Margin controls the space outside an element’s border, separating it from neighbors. It does not affect the element’s internal content (use padding for that). Margins can collapse in edge cases and affect layout flow; test nested elements and breakpoints.
followUpConfusion: Margins can collapse in edge cases and affect layout flow; test nested elements and breakpoints.

realWorldUseCases: MDN defines margin thoroughly, but it is lighter on the practical spacing decision developers usually need to make between outer separation and safer layout primitives.
actionableExamples: It includes canonical syntax examples and collapse coverage, so the example criterion stays competitive.
followUpConfusion: Our page is more explicit about the follow-up confusion teams hit first: collapsing margins, nested layout surprises, and when gap or padding is the better tool.

actionableExamples: Keep one shorter outer-spacing example so the page stays faster to scan than a full property reference.

competitors.winReason: MDN is the canonical margin reference, but our page is stronger as an interview answer because it turns margin into a concrete outside-spacing, collapse, and layout-flow debugging decision.

followUpConfusion: Margins can collapse in edge cases and affect layout flow; test nested elements and breakpoints.

realWorldUseCases: GreatFrontEnd is relevant for CSS prep, but the hub page is less explicit about the exact collapse and spacing-tool trade-offs behind this property.
actionableExamples: It remains a strong interview bank, so the worked-example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: Our page is more direct on the likely follow-up: outer spacing is not internal spacing, and collapse makes vertical rhythm trickier than it first looks.

competitors.label: margin

theirStrengths: It is strong on margin collapse, negative margins, and practical property behavior beyond the raw syntax.
theirGaps: It is less compact as a single interview answer and less explicit about when margin should give way to gap or padding.

realWorldUseCases: Margin controls the space outside an element’s border, separating it from neighbors. It does not affect the element’s internal content (use padding for that). Margins can collapse in edge cases and affect layout flow; test nested elements and breakpoints.
followUpConfusion: Margins can collapse in edge cases and affect layout flow; test nested elements and breakpoints.

realWorldUseCases: CSS-Tricks is strong on margin behavior, but our answer is more compact about the production decision between outer spacing, collapse risk, and alternate layout primitives.
